headlines.title: Japan’s Nikkei hits record high, yen steady after BOJ hikes rates as expected
Asian shares are mostly higher and Japan’s Nikkei tops 70,000 before BOJ rate hike
Asian shares are mostly higher and Japan's Nikkei tops 70,000 before BOJ rate hike
Interest rates hit a 31-year high! The Bank of Japan raised rates by 25 basis points and paused its bond-buying taper, with the Deputy Governor's substitute press conference becoming a focal point.
Nikkei tops 70,000 for first time as BOJ rate hike boosts confidence in Japan's economy

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 닛케이 225 지수가 사상 처음으로 70,000선을 돌파하며 역사적인 기록을 세웠습니다. 이는 일본은행(BOJ)이 예상대로 기준금리를 25bp 인상하여 31년 만에 최고 수준으로 올리고 채권 매입 축소를 일시 중단한 가운데 나타났습니다. BOJ의 금리 인상은 일본 경제에 대한 신뢰를 높이는 요인으로 작용했으며, 엔화는 안정적인 흐름을 보였습니다. 한편, 부총재의 대리 기자회견에도 시장의 관심이 집중되었습니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese stock market experienced a historic day, with the Nikkei 225 index soaring to a new record high, topping 70,000 for the first time. This rally occurred as the Bank of Japan (BOJ) raised interest rates by 25 basis points, marking a 31-year high, and paused its bond-buying taper. The BOJ's largely anticipated move boosted confidence in Japan's economy, while the yen remained steady. Attention was also drawn to the Deputy Governor's substitute press conference.
